title: MA-1 MK III

infobox.value: * [Bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [assault rifle](/wiki/Assault_rifle "Assault rifle") (MA-1 Mk. III) * Bullpup [light machine gun](/wiki/Light_machine_gun "Light machine gun") (MA-2 Mk. III) * Bullpup [carbine](/wiki/Carbine "Carbine") (MA-3 Mk. III)
[Myanmar Directorate of Defence Industries](/wiki/Myanmar_Directorate_of_Defence_Industries "Myanmar Directorate of Defence Industries")
* Hooded post front sight and aperture rear sight (Standard) * Y/MA 95-002 telescopic sight (Optional)

is a family of Myanmar-made [Bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [assault rifles](/wiki/Assault_rifle "Assault rifle") chambered for [5.56×45mm NATO](/wiki/5.56%C3%9745mm_NATO "5.56×45mm NATO"), based on the QBZ-97s that were exported to Myanmar in 2009.[[3]](#cite_note-BT-4)[[4]](#cite_note-SP-5) Despite claims that Myanmar made them as an indigenous weapon, the MA-1 Mk. IIIs were made without any licensing agreements with China.[[5]](#cite_note-Parade-6)[[6]](#cite_note-ME-7)
Minor adaptions were made to the original Chinese design.

["Toys for the Boys"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160623173548/http://asia-pacific-solidarity.net/asiapacific/focus/at_toysfortheboysinmyanmar_060911.htm). *Asia Pacific Solidarity Network*. Archived from [the original](http://asia-pacific-solidarity.net/asiapacific/focus/at_toysfortheboysinmyanmar_060911.htm) on 2016-06-23. Retrieved 2018-07-26.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [MA-1 MK III](/w/index.php?title=MA-1_MK_III&redirect=no "MA-1 MK III"))

2012 Bullpup assault rifle made in Myanmar

The **DI MA-1 Mk. III** is a family of Myanmar-made [Bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [assault rifles](/wiki/Assault_rifle "Assault rifle") chambered for [5.56×45mm NATO](/wiki/5.56%C3%9745mm_NATO "5.56×45mm NATO"), based on the QBZ-97s that were exported to Myanmar in 2009.[[3]](#cite_note-BT-4)[[4]](#cite_note-SP-5) Despite claims that Myanmar made them as an indigenous weapon, the MA-1 Mk. IIIs were made without any licensing agreements with China.[[5]](#cite_note-Parade-6)[[6]](#cite_note-ME-7)

The MA designation on the weapon means **Myanmar Army**.[[7]](#cite_note-APS-8)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Myanmar decided to redesign a bullpup rifle after [Tatmadaw](/wiki/Tatmadaw "Tatmadaw") troops reported multiple failures with the [EMER-K1](/wiki/EMER-K1 "EMER-K1"), an earlier bullpup rifle clone of the QBZ-97, ranging from gun jams to stoppages.[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-9) At the time, some of them were issued to Tatmadaw forces on patrol duties at the [Yadana gas field](/wiki/Yadana_gas_field "Yadana gas field") in 1998.[[9]](#cite_note-BMSA-10)[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-9) Myanmar originally wanted to acquire QBZ-97s and eventually set up a production line to manufacture them on their own, but China refused to honor them due to arms sanctions against the [State Peace and Development Council](/wiki/State_Peace_and_Development_Council "State Peace and Development Council").[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-9)[[3]](#cite_note-BT-4) Subsequently, the QBZ-97s previously sold to Myanmar were not returned.[[1]](#cite_note-GAA-1)

The QBZ-97 rifles with Myanmar were [reverse engineered](/wiki/Reverse_engineering "Reverse engineering") again; they did not seek Chinese assistance in creating the MA-1 Mk. IIIs.[[6]](#cite_note-ME-7)[[5]](#cite_note-Parade-6) During analysis of the rifles in 2009,[[4]](#cite_note-SP-5) they were reported to be suitable for the Tatmadaw in jungle operations.[[10]](#cite_note-TWGD-11)

The MA-1 Mk. IIIs were first shown in public in 2012.[[11]](#cite_note-12) They were subsequently shown at various Tatmadaw [Armed Forces Day](/wiki/Armed_Forces_Day_(Myanmar) "Armed Forces Day (Myanmar)") parades.[[12]](#cite_note-13)

Plans were made to fully adopt the MA-1 Mk. III by 2016, but it was delayed due to quality issues.[[10]](#cite_note-TWGD-11) At the time, it was reported that the MA-1 Mk. IIIs had poor reliability in the field.[[13]](#cite_note-KKC-14) Chinese media criticized Myanmar for cloning the QBZ-97 without permission after the rifle was publicly revealed.[[14]](#cite_note-15)

In 2020, Myanma social media photos showed an improved version of the MA-3 Mk. III, implying that production issues were mostly resolved.[[15]](#cite_note-16)

## Design

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Design")]

According to the Special Advisory Council for Myanmar (SAC-M), the rifle family is reportedly being manufactured at DDI's KaPaSa 22 factory.[[16]](#cite_note-Fatal-17)

While the MA-1 Mk. III has visual similarities with the QBZ-97, the difference lies with the use of phenolic plastic materials instead of engineered plastics, which gives off the brown finish.[[5]](#cite_note-Parade-6)[[17]](#cite_note-PD-18) Another difference is the shape of the handguard and the vent holes on it.[[18]](#cite_note-BTM-19) The MA-1 Mk. IIIs use waffle-based 30-round STANAG magazines.[[2]](#cite_note-mt-3)

The MA-1 Mk. IIIs do not use the same Type 95 bayonet made for the QBZ-95.[[19]](#cite_note-20)[[20]](#cite_note-21) Instead, the Tatmadaw uses a clone of the Type 81 bayonet for the rifles.[[5]](#cite_note-Parade-6) Only the MA-1/3 Mk. III have bayonet lugs installed as a default option.[[10]](#cite_note-TWGD-11)

For the MA-3 Mk. III, some of the more recently made carbines have carry handles that have a more rectangular shape, more ergonomic front handguard and vent holes, making it resemble the QBZ-97.[[21]](#cite_note-22)

## Variants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Variants")]

### MA-1 Mk. III

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: MA-1 Mk. III")]

The standard assault rifle variant based on the QBZ-97.[[22]](#cite_note-23)

### MA-2 Mk. III

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: MA-2 Mk. III")]

The light machine gun variant with a long barrel and a bipod.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

### MA-3 Mk. III

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: MA-3 Mk. III")]

A carbine variant.[[24]](#cite_note-ZM-25) Known to be used by Myanma special forces units.[[25]](#cite_note-BT2-26)

### MA-4 Mk. III

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: MA-4 Mk. III")]

A variant of the MA-1 Mk. III equipped with an underbarrel grenade launcher.[[26]](#cite_note-27) It is also known to be used by Myanmar special forces units.[[25]](#cite_note-BT2-26)

## Users

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Users")]

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Flag_of_Myanmar.svg/40px-Flag_of_Myanmar.svg.png) [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"): Tatmadaw,[[24]](#cite_note-ZM-25) including the Myanmar Navy SEALs.[[27]](#cite_note-28)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=DI_MA-1_Mk._III&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Minor adaptions were made to the original Chinese design.
